name: Shrimp Fajitas
description: Shrimp fajitas are the perfect quick and easy meal! Made from sautéed bell peppers and onions, succulent shrimp, and fajita seasoning.
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ pounds large shrimp, (peeled and deveined)
1 teaspoon kosher salt, (plus more to taste)
½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions: In a large bowl, add the shrimp, garlic, chili powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, salt, cumin, black pepper, and 1 ½ tablespoons of olive oil. Mix together to combine and set aside.

Heat remaining 1 ½ tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add peppers and onions and cook, stirring occasionally, for 13-15 minutes until peppers have softened and onions have started to caramelize.
Transfer the cooked peppers and onions to a bowl, cover with a plate, and set aside.
Add the seasoned shrimp and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes or until the shrimp are pink and no longer translucent.
Add the cooked peppers and onions back into the skillet, and top with lime juice and chopped cilantro. Mix everything together, taste, and season with more salt if necessary.
Remove from heat and serve in warm flour tortillas with sour cream, avocado slices, and other desired toppings.

ai.cliffNotes: Bloom the spice mixture with oil and shrimp first, then cook vegetables separately to control their texture before combining everything at the end.
keyTips: Cook peppers and onions for 13-15 minutes without the shrimp so they caramelize properly while shrimp stays tender
Add lime juice and cilantro after combining everything back together to brighten flavors without cooking them off
Don't skip the initial marinating step—coating shrimp in seasoned oil for even flavor distribution is critical
Use medium-high heat for vegetables to develop color and caramelization, not just steam them
